generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) integrated with a cycloidal gearbox for high-precision positional control in manufacturing environments"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox providing precise positional control and torque amplification for the industrial robot system"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor for real-time spatial feedback of motor displacement"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox for spatial distribution and displacement verification"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance CombinedPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionData, GearboxPositionData
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-physics-aware friction identification model using aggregated sensor data traces for predictive maintenance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowFramework
]

instance MotionAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for motion accuracy validation using SysML state machines and MontiArc automata for closed-loop control simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SiemensSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance AADLArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Architecture description using AADL for component and connector perspectives defining system interfaces and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AssetAdministrationShell
]

instance SysMLInteractionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Interaction Block Diagram (IBD) representing system components, interfaces, and interconnections for manufacturing automation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowFramework
]

instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Abstracted time-series sensor data traces aggregated from physical encoders for contextualized manufacturing monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CombinedPositionData, InputCurrentData, TorqueData, AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowFramework
]

instance FrictionAnalysisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed friction-related parameters derived from raw sensor data for predictive maintenance and operational monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionBehaviorModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SiemensSinumerik840DSLController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller providing real-time motion control and interfacing with the Digital Twin via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionBehaviorModel, MotionAccuracyModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight publish-subscribe messaging protocol enabling bidirectional data flow between physical sensors and digital representations"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowFramework
]

instance DigitalShadowFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "One-way automated data flow system aggregating and abstracting physical sensor data for manufacturing context-specific analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContextualDataTrace, FrictionAnalysisData
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized framework integrating robot components (motors, encoders) into a unified digital representation for interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:enables AADLArchitectureModel
]

instance SiemensSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for behavioral simulation and validation of motion control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyModel
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous multi-physics-aware identification of joint friction using aggregated contextual data traces for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance FrictionLossDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detection of anomalies in friction-related parameters (torque, velocity deviations) to ensure motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance MotionAccuracyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of motion accuracy deviations through friction analysis and encoder feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface for monitoring friction-related metrics and system performance trends for maintenance technicians and supervisors"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDashboard
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance insights derived from friction loss detection and behavior modeling for proactive system health monitoring"
]

instance OperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational performance metrics including motion accuracy trends and friction-related anomalies"
]

instance OperationalDashboard : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-generated visualization of system health metrics for maintenance decision-making and performance optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert system triggered by detected friction anomalies requiring maintenance intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for process adjustments to improve motion accuracy and reduce friction losses"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment
instance IndustrialRobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance ManufacturingProcessEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DigitalShadowFramework, AssetAdministrationShell, SiemensSinumerik840DSLController
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) system** is conceptualized as an extension of the **Digital Shadow**, which operates as a **one-way data flow** from the physical system to a digital representation. The **orchestration** of the DT system is grounded in **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, leveraging **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** to model behavior. The **system-wide coordination** is implicitly supported by **active software components** that process data from digital shadows, enabling autonomous operations and real-time synchronization. The **resource management** is implied through the integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., **C++/Java**) to refine and validate DT models. The absence of explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) in the documents suggests a **hybrid centralized-decentralized approach**, where core coordination is handled by MBSE frameworks and ADLs, supplemented by tool-specific workflows."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context spans structured modeling phases aligned with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, particularly emphasizing **multi-level abstraction** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). Key stages include:
  - **Development Phase**: Utilizes **SysML** and **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML IBDs** to define components, interfaces, and interconnections via component & connector perspectives. **Behavioral modeling** is supported through **MontiArc Automata** or **state machines** in SysML v1.7, often integrated with simulation tools such as **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages like **C++/Java**.
  - **Data Aggregation & Abstraction**: Digital Shadows (DS) serve as foundational data traces, aggregating contextual information for specific purposes, while DTs enable **active processing** (e.g., software components interpreting data) to evolve beyond passive DS roles.
  **Transition Management**: The shift from DS to DT involves integrating **active components** (e.g., software systems) to enable bidirectional data flow, governance likely follows **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SMArDT) to ensure alignment across heterogeneous stakeholders’ perspectives.
  **Lifecycle Governance**: Emphasizes **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool/model notation compatibility** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java) to maintain consistency across abstraction layers.
 "
]

  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin technical connection** leverages **MQTT** for unidirectional data streaming, as explicitly stated in the document. The network topology is not detailed, but the system operates within a **cloud/edge hybrid architecture** (implied by the passive nature and potential integration with AAS), though no specific latency or bandwidth requirements are specified.

  **Key Technical Specifications**:
  - **Communication Protocol**: MQTT (for one-way data flow).
  - **Reliability/Redundancy**: Not explicitly mentioned, but the passive nature suggests minimal redundancy is required for data transmission.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not detailed; the document does not specify encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms for the data flow.
  - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**: Implied for structured data representation and integration, though their role in the technical connection is not explicitly described.
"
]

    base:desc "
  The **Digital Shadow hosting and deployment** is described as an **executable system** designed to be passive, with no explicit mention of cloud, edge, or on-premise deployment environments. The document references **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** as a foundational component for the DS, suggesting a modular and potentially cloud-based deployment model.

  **Key Technical Specifications**:
  - **Hosting Environment**: Not explicitly specified (no mention of cloud/edge/on-premise), but the context implies a **scalable, real-time infrastructure** due to the streaming nature of data.
  - **Computational/Storage Requirements**: Not detailed; the document does not provide specific hardware or software constraints.
  - **Deployment Models**: Not specified; the DS is described as a standalone executable, but no mention of CI/CD pipelines, containerization (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes), or orchestration is present.
  - **Availability/Reliability**: Not detailed; the DS is passive and does not imply high-availability guarantees.
  - **Dashboard Integration**: Implied for visualization, though no specific tools (e.g., Grafana, Tableau) or deployment details are mentioned.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  The provided documents reference **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML IBDs**, which define structural and behavioral models for Digital Twin systems. **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** are mentioned in the broader context (Abstract) but are not explicitly detailed in the extracted sections. **Interoperability standards** are implied through ADLs but not formally specified. **Certification requirements** or compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are **not addressed**, and no explicit interoperability protocols (e.g., OPC UA) are mentioned.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **physical safety mechanism** for robotic operations, specifically leveraging a **safety cell** that restricts the operational range of the robotic arm (e.g., MABI Max-100) to a defined angular workspace of **[−90°, 90°]**, ensuring operator safety by preventing unintended collisions. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols, the system’s integration with **IoT platforms** (e.g., Thingsboard) implies potential exposure to network-based threats, necessitating adherence to **MQTT protocol** (as referenced in Document 52) for secure communication between devices. The system’s compliance with **RAMI 4.0** (Document 48, 49) suggests adherence to safety-critical standards, though no explicit mention of **ISO 13849** or other cybersecurity frameworks is documented. The Digital Twin’s reliance on **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (Document 50) for asset modeling may also imply standardized safety and security governance frameworks, though specific details on fail-safe mechanisms or threat mitigation are absent."
]
